purpose: Per-package standard route truth with an explicit classification on every field. Consumed by knowledge-catalog-jvto-bootstrap's customer-sales projection and the WhatsApp runtime so a fact is never presented above its evidence.

classification_taxonomy.final_jvto_standard: fixed operating policy, independent of date/conditions
classification_taxonomy.source_backed_estimate: directionally reliable but not guaranteed (operator seed / mapbox routing)
classification_taxonomy.live_condition: only valid at request time; never asserted statically (requires a live tool)
classification_taxonomy.exception: package-specific deviation from a shared JVTO rule
classification_taxonomy.absent: no source rule and no shared JVTO rule of same scope (recorded as a gap; surfaced as handoff, never invented)

bali_transfer.note: Bali-origin: Gilimanuk→Ketapang sea crossing at the START; the tour then finishes in Surabaya (NOT a finish in Bali).

weather_advisory.value: Mount Bromo: difficulty moderate (physical demand 3/5). Weather by season — Dry season (Apr-Sep): Clear skies, excellent sunrise visibility. Wet season (Oct-Mar): Frequent fog, rain, and limited visibility. Cold temperatures year-round at high altitude. Rainfall — Moderate during wet season (100-200mm/month), Low during dry season (20-50mm/month) Key risk (weather, medium): Wear multiple warm layers, gloves, and beanie.
weather_advisory.weather_by_season: Dry season (Apr-Sep): Clear skies, excellent sunrise visibility. Wet season (Oct-Mar): Frequent fog, rain, and limited visibility. Cold temperatures year-round at high altitude.
weather_advisory.rainfall_intensity: Moderate during wet season (100-200mm/month), Low during dry season (20-50mm/month)

risk_factors.description: Extreme cold at sunrise viewpoints
risk_factors.mitigation: Wear multiple warm layers, gloves, and beanie.

risk_factors.description: Volcanic ash and sand can be slippery
risk_factors.mitigation: Wear sturdy shoes and watch footing on stairs.

risk_factors.description: Altitude effects for some visitors
risk_factors.mitigation: Take time to acclimatize, stay hydrated.

risk_factors.description: High visitor density at sunrise viewpoints

weather_advisory.value: Ijen Crater: difficulty hard (physical demand 5/5). Weather by season — Dry season (Apr-Oct): Clear skies, minimal fog. Wet season (Nov-Mar): Frequent rain, dense fog, higher gas concentration. Rainfall — High during wet season (200-400mm/month), Low during dry season (50-100mm/month) Key risk (health, high): Use provided gas masks, limit time near active vents, immediate descent if feeling unwell.
weather_advisory.weather_by_season: Dry season (Apr-Oct): Clear skies, minimal fog. Wet season (Nov-Mar): Frequent rain, dense fog, higher gas concentration.
weather_advisory.rainfall_intensity: High during wet season (200-400mm/month), Low during dry season (50-100mm/month)

risk_factors.description: Toxic sulfur gas exposure causing respiratory issues
risk_factors.mitigation: Use provided gas masks, limit time near active vents, immediate descent if feeling unwell.

risk_factors.description: Steep, slippery paths especially in darkness
risk_factors.mitigation: Use headlamps, trekking poles, and proper footwear. Follow guide closely.

risk_factors.description: Rapid weather changes and cold temperatures at night
risk_factors.mitigation: Bring warm layers, rain jacket, and check weather forecast.

risk_factors.description: High altitude and physical exertion
risk_factors.mitigation: Guests must disclose relevant medical conditions.

staging.label: Bromo area sunrise staging overnight
operational_notes: early jeep pickup
takeaway breakfast possible
cold-weather preparation

staging.label: Banyuwangi staging overnight (Bali-origin Ijen packages)
operational_notes: Luminor Hotel / Banyuwangi area used for Bali-origin packages
ferry from Gilimanuk requires Ferizy pre-booking
arrive Banyuwangi by 18:00–20:00 recommended for adequate Ijen rest
medical check and briefing arranged at hotel
midnight departure to Ijen from here
risk_if_arrival_late: reduced rest before midnight Ijen departure

exceptions.value: east_to_west (reverse of the standard Surabaya west→east corridor)
exceptions.note: Bali-origin tours run the corridor in reverse of the standard Surabaya order; feasibility/staging follow the reversed sequence.

route_recommendations.note: Standard route already follows the recommended Bromo→Ijen→Ketapang/Bali order, avoiding the documented backtracking risk.

Ferizy ticket must be pre-booked H-1 (geofence-gated 2.65km from Ketapang). Allow 2–3h harbor queue buffer especially during peak / holiday periods. Confirm vehicle-crossing or guest-only handoff preference before booking.

Ijen has a daily visitor cap (~2000/day). Online ticket required at QRIS-only cashless since Jan 2025. Access can close without notice for volcanic activity or authority decision. Gas mask and medical check are mandatory on-site. Confirm ticket availability before finalizing Ijen date.

bali_transfer.note: Bali-origin: Gilimanuk→Ketapang sea crossing at the START; the tour then finishes in Surabaya (NOT a finish in Bali).

weather_advisory.value: Madakaripura Waterfall: difficulty moderate (physical demand 3/5). Weather by season — Dry season (May-Oct): Optimal conditions with minimal rain, clear water flow. Wet season (Nov-Apr): Heavy rainfall increases flash flood risk, stronger water currents. Guides are trained in river safety.
weather_advisory.weather_by_season: Dry season (May-Oct): Optimal conditions with minimal rain, clear water flow. Wet season (Nov-Apr): Heavy rainfall increases flash flood risk, stronger water currents.
weather_advisory.rainfall_intensity: High during wet season (300-500mm/month), Moderate during dry season (50-150mm/month)

risk_factors.description: Flash floods during heavy rainfall
risk_factors.mitigation: Guides are trained in river safety.

risk_factors.description: Slippery rocks and uneven riverbed
risk_factors.mitigation: Guides assist with difficult sections.

risk_factors.description: Slips and falls on wet surfaces
risk_factors.mitigation: Safety briefing provided.

weather_advisory.value: Papuma Beach: difficulty easy (physical demand 1/5). Weather by season — Dry season (Apr-Oct): Clear skies, calm waters, ideal for beach activities. Wet season (Nov-Mar): Higher waves, occasional rain, stronger currents. Rainfall — Moderate during wet season (150-300mm/month), Low during dry season (30-100mm/month) Key risk (marine, medium): Swim only in designated safe areas. Follow lifeguard instructions if available.
weather_advisory.weather_by_season: Dry season (Apr-Oct): Clear skies, calm waters, ideal for beach activities. Wet season (Nov-Mar): Higher waves, occasional rain, stronger currents.
weather_advisory.rainfall_intensity: Moderate during wet season (150-300mm/month), Low during dry season (30-100mm/month)

risk_factors.description: Strong currents and powerful waves
risk_factors.mitigation: Swim only in designated safe areas. Follow lifeguard instructions if available.

risk_factors.description: Slippery rocks when wet
risk_factors.mitigation: Wear water shoes with good grip on rocky surfaces

risk_factors.description: Strong sun exposure
risk_factors.mitigation: Use sunscreen, hat, and seek shade during peak hours

weather_advisory.value: Tumpak Sewu Waterfall: difficulty hard (physical demand 4/5). Weather by season — Dry season (May-Oct): Best conditions with minimal rain, clear skies. Wet season (Nov-Apr): Heavy rainfall increases water flow and flash flood risk. Descent is controlled and assisted.
weather_advisory.weather_by_season: Dry season (May-Oct): Best conditions with minimal rain, clear skies. Wet season (Nov-Apr): Heavy rainfall increases water flow and flash flood risk.
weather_advisory.rainfall_intensity: High during wet season (300-600mm/month), Moderate during dry season (50-200mm/month)

risk_factors.description: Steep and slippery descent with rope sections
risk_factors.mitigation: Descent is controlled and assisted.

risk_factors.description: Flash floods during rainfall
risk_factors.mitigation: Emergency evacuation plans in place.

risk_factors.description: Physical exertion and potential for slips/falls
risk_factors.mitigation: Safety briefing and physical assessment.

route_recommendations.note: Standard route already follows the recommended Bromo→Ijen→Ketapang/Bali order, avoiding the documented backtracking risk.

Ferizy ticket must be pre-booked H-1 (geofence-gated 2.65km from Ketapang). Allow 2–3h harbor queue buffer especially during peak / holiday periods. Confirm vehicle-crossing or guest-only handoff preference before booking.

Ijen has a daily visitor cap (~2000/day). Online ticket required at QRIS-only cashless since Jan 2025. Access can close without notice for volcanic activity or authority decision. Gas mask and medical check are mandatory on-site. Confirm ticket availability before finalizing Ijen date.

bali_transfer.note: Route stays within East Java; no Bali sea crossing.

weather_advisory.value: Ijen Crater: difficulty hard (physical demand 5/5). Weather by season — Dry season (Apr-Oct): Clear skies, minimal fog. Wet season (Nov-Mar): Frequent rain, dense fog, higher gas concentration. Rainfall — High during wet season (200-400mm/month), Low during dry season (50-100mm/month) Key risk (health, high): Use provided gas masks, limit time near active vents, immediate descent if feeling unwell.

risk_factors.mitigation: Use provided gas masks, limit time near active vents, immediate descent if feeling unwell.

risk_factors.mitigation: Use headlamps, trekking poles, and proper footwear. Follow guide closely.

risk_factors.mitigation: Bring warm layers, rain jacket, and check weather forecast.

coastal road is rain-sensitive; check weather
not suitable before a tight airport or train deadline

risk_factors.description: Strong currents and powerful waves
risk_factors.mitigation: Swim only in designated safe areas. Follow lifeguard instructions if available.

risk_factors.description: Slippery rocks when wet
risk_factors.mitigation: Wear water shoes with good grip on rocky surfaces

risk_factors.description: Strong sun exposure
risk_factors.mitigation: Use sunscreen, hat, and seek shade during peak hours

weather_advisory.value: Tumpak Sewu Waterfall: difficulty hard (physical demand 4/5). Weather by season — Dry season (May-Oct): Best conditions with minimal rain, clear skies. Wet season (Nov-Apr): Heavy rainfall increases water flow and flash flood risk. Descent is controlled and assisted.
weather_advisory.weather_by_season: Dry season (May-Oct): Best conditions with minimal rain, clear skies. Wet season (Nov-Apr): Heavy rainfall increases water flow and flash flood risk.
weather_advisory.rainfall_intensity: High during wet season (300-600mm/month), Moderate during dry season (50-200mm/month)

risk_factors.description: Steep and slippery descent with rope sections
risk_factors.mitigation: Descent is controlled and assisted.

risk_factors.description: Flash floods during rainfall
risk_factors.mitigation: Emergency evacuation plans in place.

risk_factors.description: Physical exertion and potential for slips/falls
risk_factors.mitigation: Safety briefing and physical assessment.

operational_notes: early jeep pickup
takeaway breakfast possible
cold-weather preparation

staging.label: Bondowoso / Ijen staging overnight
operational_notes: medical check can be arranged at hotel
dinner before Ijen preparation
midnight departure to Ijen

staging.label: Tumpak Sewu / Lumajang staging overnight
operational_notes: Artha Cottage / Lumajang area is the closest base
early morning start recommended 07:00–08:00 for waterfall access
waterfall descent takes 1–2h; weather-sensitive — plan around rain
local guide mandatory
risk_if_arrival_late: rain risk increases with later start

staging.label: Papuma / Jember staging overnight
operational_notes: Doho Homestay / Jember area is the closest base
Papuma best visited in morning before crowds
coastal road is rain-sensitive; check weather
not suitable before a tight airport or train deadline

staging.label: Malang / Batu staging overnight (extended packages)
operational_notes: Whiz Prime Malang / Holiday Inn Express Surabaya used for city stays
Malang city and Batu attractions accessible on day-use basis
suitable for leisure day after highland activities
Surabaya airport accessible 2–4h from Malang depending on traffic
risk_if_arrival_late: Surabaya airport deadline risk if traffic

route_recommendations.note: Ijen has a daily visitor cap (~2000/day). Online ticket required at QRIS-only cashless since Jan 2025. Access can close without notice for volcanic activity or authority decision. Gas mask and medical check are mandatory on-site. Confirm ticket availability before finalizing Ijen date.

bali_transfer.note: Route stays within East Java; no Bali sea crossing.

weather_advisory.value: Mount Bromo: difficulty moderate (physical demand 3/5). Weather by season — Dry season (Apr-Sep): Clear skies, excellent sunrise visibility. Wet season (Oct-Mar): Frequent fog, rain, and limited visibility. Cold temperatures year-round at high altitude. Rainfall — Moderate during wet season (100-200mm/month), Low during dry season (20-50mm/month) Key risk (weather, medium): Wear multiple warm layers, gloves, and beanie.
weather_advisory.weather_by_season: Dry season (Apr-Sep): Clear skies, excellent sunrise visibility. Wet season (Oct-Mar): Frequent fog, rain, and limited visibility. Cold temperatures year-round at high altitude.
weather_advisory.rainfall_intensity: Moderate during wet season (100-200mm/month), Low during dry season (20-50mm/month)

risk_factors.description: Extreme cold at sunrise viewpoints
risk_factors.mitigation: Wear multiple warm layers, gloves, and beanie.

risk_factors.description: Volcanic ash and sand can be slippery
risk_factors.mitigation: Wear sturdy shoes and watch footing on stairs.

risk_factors.description: Altitude effects for some visitors
risk_factors.mitigation: Take time to acclimatize, stay hydrated.

risk_factors.description: High visitor density at sunrise viewpoints

weather_advisory.value: Madakaripura Waterfall: difficulty moderate (physical demand 3/5). Weather by season — Dry season (May-Oct): Optimal conditions with minimal rain, clear water flow. Wet season (Nov-Apr): Heavy rainfall increases flash flood risk, stronger water currents. Guides are trained in river safety.
weather_advisory.weather_by_season: Dry season (May-Oct): Optimal conditions with minimal rain, clear water flow. Wet season (Nov-Apr): Heavy rainfall increases flash flood risk, stronger water currents.
weather_advisory.rainfall_intensity: High during wet season (300-500mm/month), Moderate during dry season (50-150mm/month)

risk_factors.description: Flash floods during heavy rainfall
risk_factors.mitigation: Guides are trained in river safety.

risk_factors.description: Slippery rocks and uneven riverbed
risk_factors.mitigation: Guides assist with difficult sections.

risk_factors.description: Slips and falls on wet surfaces
risk_factors.mitigation: Safety briefing provided.

exceptions.note: Specialty package: substitutes a standard volcano-first leg (e.g. Taman Safari) and does not follow the default destination set.

bali_transfer.note: Finishes toward Bali via a Ketapang/Gilimanuk ferry crossing; the onward Bali hotel transfer is a live arrangement, not a direct hotel drop unless stated.

weather_advisory.value: Ijen Crater: difficulty hard (physical demand 5/5). Weather by season — Dry season (Apr-Oct): Clear skies, minimal fog. Wet season (Nov-Mar): Frequent rain, dense fog, higher gas concentration. Rainfall — High during wet season (200-400mm/month), Low during dry season (50-100mm/month) Key risk (health, high): Use provided gas masks, limit time near active vents, immediate descent if feeling unwell.
weather_advisory.weather_by_season: Dry season (Apr-Oct): Clear skies, minimal fog. Wet season (Nov-Mar): Frequent rain, dense fog, higher gas concentration.
weather_advisory.rainfall_intensity: High during wet season (200-400mm/month), Low during dry season (50-100mm/month)

risk_factors.description: Toxic sulfur gas exposure causing respiratory issues
risk_factors.mitigation: Use provided gas masks, limit time near active vents, immediate descent if feeling unwell.

risk_factors.description: Steep, slippery paths especially in darkness
risk_factors.mitigation: Use headlamps, trekking poles, and proper footwear. Follow guide closely.

risk_factors.description: Rapid weather changes and cold temperatures at night
risk_factors.mitigation: Bring warm layers, rain jacket, and check weather forecast.

risk_factors.description: High altitude and physical exertion
risk_factors.mitigation: Guests must disclose relevant medical conditions.

route_recommendations.note: Standard route already follows the recommended Bromo→Ijen→Ketapang/Bali order, avoiding the documented backtracking risk.

Ferizy ticket must be pre-booked H-1 (geofence-gated 2.65km from Ketapang). Allow 2–3h harbor queue buffer especially during peak / holiday periods. Confirm vehicle-crossing or guest-only handoff preference before booking.

Ijen has a daily visitor cap (~2000/day). Online ticket required at QRIS-only cashless since Jan 2025. Access can close without notice for volcanic activity or authority decision. Gas mask and medical check are mandatory on-site. Confirm ticket availability before finalizing Ijen date.
